memory:memory/2026-04-03.md:1:30.snippet: # 2026-04-03 ## Morning Idea — What-If Reframe - **Topic:** Defer sync mode choice out of onboarding - **Angle:** What-if reframe (from March 31 persona reviews) - **Idea:** Remove sync mode selection from onboarding entirely. Default everyone to Summary mode, then surface Per-Transaction as an upgrade prompt after first successful sync — when users have context to decide. Eliminates the scariest moment in onboarding (all 3 personas flagged "requires new org" as conversion killer). - **Built on:** March 31 persona panel — Viktor rated sync mode selection as #1 universal finding, Mike gave 40-50% quit risk --- ## End-of-Day Progress Snapshot (23:00 Vilnius) ### What Happened Today - Light

memory:memory/2026-03-31.md:1:27.snippet: # 2026-03-31 — Persona Panel System ## What Happened - Ignat asked about creating sub-agents for UX testing - Built a 3-persona review system: - 🧮 **Sarah Chen** — CPA, 15yr, 40 clients, QBO power user. Reviews accounting terminology, multi-client workflows, data integrity - 🛍️ **Mike Torres** — Solo ecommerce founder, zero accounting knowledge, impatient, jargon-anxious. Reviews comprehension, anxiety triggers, quit risks - 😤 **Viktor Harsch** — Brutal senior UX designer, 20yr ex-Google/Booking/Revolut. Full UX audit with impact×effort priority matrix - Tested all 3 on the onboarding prototype (https://dashasyn.github.io/synder-prototypes/onboarding/) - Sub-agent spawning lessons:

memory:memory/2026-03-31.md:21:58.snippet: ## Key Findings (Onboarding Prototype v4) ### 🔴 Universal (all 3 flagged) 1. Sync mode = conversion killer ("requires new org" terrifies users, 40-50% quit risk for non-accountants) 2. Jargon never explained (reconcile, journal entries, COGS) 3. Revenue field feels invasive — no explanation of why it's needed ### Accountant-specific - No multi-client scalability info - Missing Chart of Accounts mapping - Landing page ignores accountant audience - No cash vs accrual question ### Business owner-specific - Sage Intacct/NetSuite make small sellers feel "wrong place" - "Select all that apply" missing on platforms - "Intuit" SSO — doesn't know that's QuickBooks ### Quick wins (Viktor) - Add "~

memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:68:82.snippet: - Dynamic panel label: "Missing in Synder" vs "Missing in accounting" based on accounting platform - Zero tooltips on: tabs, column headers, filter fields, Ignore, Process log, Export - **Viktor (UX designer) subagent review** of the prototype - Verdict: "Iterate hard" — 5 critical flaws found - Best thing in the proto: column mapping error detection (ships a support ticket saver) - Worst flaw: "Match all →" button is a phantom action violating QuickBooks' 20-year match-by-click mental model - Other criticals: shared filter bar has undefined scope, progress bar is feel-good noise, "Mark as expected" is undefined jargon, insight buried below the fold - Top 3 prod-blocking fixes:

memory:memory/2026-04-10.md:19:39.snippet: - Audited every button, tooltip, hover on live Synder page - **Critical findings:** - "Refresh data" button tooltip: "Create a new reconciliation..." — label says refresh, action is create-new (mismatch) - Description column uses hover-only info icons — hides "STRIPE PAYOUT" / "Subscription update" behind 25+ tiny icons per page - No tooltips on: Process log, Export, any tab, any column header, any filter field, Ignore button - Ignore is one-click, no confirmation, no undo toast - Dynamic panel label: "Missing in Synder" vs "Missing in accounting" based on accounting platform - **Documented:** Full current-logic.md with 12 issues, data model, API endpoints ### Viktor (Senior UX De
